revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mitoshiro Kofun | native_name =御年代古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mitoshiro Kofun, entrance-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mitoshiro Kofun entrance | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mitoshiro Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mitoshiro Kofun'''|御年代古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Hongō, Hiroshima|Hongō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Mihara, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important burial mounds from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue ware]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue ware pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun
{{small|at Tokyo National Museum}} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Mitoshiro Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120010.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.mihara.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/50/139872.html Mihara City home page]{{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mihara, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Aki Province]]

{{small|at Tokyo National Museum}} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Mitoshiro Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120010.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.mihara.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/50/139872.html Mihara City home page]{{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mihara, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Aki Province]]

In this area are numerous important burial mounds from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue ware]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue ware pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun
{{small|at Tokyo National Museum}} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Mitoshiro Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120010.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.mihara.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/50/139872.html Mihara City home page]{{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mihara, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Aki Province]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Mitoshiro Kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Mitoshiro Kofun | native_name =御年代古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mitoshiro Kofun, entrance-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mitoshiro Kofun entrance | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mitoshiro Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mitoshiro Kofun'''|御年代古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Hongō, Hiroshima|Hongō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Mihara, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important burial mounds from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue ware]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue ware pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

[[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue ware]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue ware pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue ware]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue ware pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳}}''|円墳||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue ware]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue ware pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important burial mounds from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue ware]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue ware pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue ware]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue ware pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Mitoshiro Kofun | native_name =御年代古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mitoshiro Kofun, entrance-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mitoshiro Kofun entrance | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mitoshiro Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mitoshiro Kofun'''|御年代古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Hongō, Hiroshima|Hongō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Mihara, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

[[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Mitoshiro Kofun | native_name =御年代古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mitoshiro Kofun, entrance-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mitoshiro Kofun entrance | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mitoshiro Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mitoshiro Kofun'''|御年代古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Hongō, Hiroshima|Hongō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Mihara, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Mitoshiro Kofun | native_name =御年代古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mitoshiro Kofun, entrance-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mitoshiro Kofun entrance | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mitoshiro Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mitoshiro Kofun'''|御年代古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Hongō, Hiroshima|Hongō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Mihara, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun
{{small|at Tokyo National Museum}} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Mitoshiro Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120010.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.mihara.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/50/139872.html Mihara City home page]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mihara, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Aki Province]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Mitoshiro Kofun]] [[en:Mitoshiro Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mitoshiro Kofun}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

[[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name =Mitoshiro Kofun | native_name =御年代古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mitoshiro Kofun, entrance-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mitoshiro Kofun entrance | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mitoshiro Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mitoshiro Kofun'''|御年代古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Hongō, Hiroshima|Hongō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Mihara, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name =Mitoshiro Kofun | native_name =御年代古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mitoshiro Kofun, entrance-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mitoshiro Kofun entrance | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mitoshiro Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mitoshiro Kofun'''|御年代古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Hongō, Hiroshima|Hongō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Mihara, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun
{{small|at Tokyo National Museum}} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Mitoshiro Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120010.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.mihara.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/50/139872.html Mihara City home page]{{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Mitoshiro Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Mitoshiro Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Mihara, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Aki Province]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Mitoshiro Kofun]] [[en:Mitoshiro Kofun]] {{citation needed|Mitoshiro Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Mitoshiro Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

[[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Mitoshiro Kofun | native_name =御年代古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mitoshiro Kofun, entrance-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mitoshiro Kofun entrance | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mitoshiro Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Mihara, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mitoshiro Kofun'''|御年代古墳|1=Mitoshiro Kofun|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Hongō, Hiroshima|Hongō]] neighborhood of the city of [[Mihara, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[burial chamber]] built with [[granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun
{{small|at Tokyo National Museum}} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Mitoshiro Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120010.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.mihara.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/50/139872.html Mihara City home page]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:History of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Aki Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Mitoshiro Kofun]] [[en:Mitoshiro Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mitoshiro Kofun}} [[ja:御年代古墳]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Mitoshiro Kofun | native_name =御年代古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mitoshiro Kofun, entrance-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mitoshiro Kofun entrance | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mitoshiro Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Mihara, Hiroshima|Mihara, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|23|52.28|N|132|56|48.77|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Mitoshiro Kofun'''|御年代古墳|1=Mitoshiro Kofun|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Hongō, Hiroshima|Hongō]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Mihara, Hiroshima|Mihara, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. In this area are numerous important Kofuns from the latter half of the Kofun period. This tumulus was discovered in 1895 during construction work for a highway. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] built with [[:en:Granite|Granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun
{{small|at Tokyo National Museum}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Mitoshiro Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-106120010.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.mihara.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/50/139872.html Mihara City home page]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Aki Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Mitoshiro Kofun]] [[en:Mitoshiro Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Mitoshiro Kofun}} [[ja:御年代古墳]]

The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] built with [[:en:Granite|Granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172985|title=御年代古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}} ==Overview== The Mitoshiro Kofun is located on the southern slope of a hill in a deep valley where the Ohara River flows into the Nuta River. The original form of the tumulus is uncertain, but it be believed to have been a circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style ''kofun'', orientated to the south. The tumulus contains a corridor-style [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] built with [[:en:Granite|Granite]] cut stone, with a total length of 10.7 meters. The burial chamber is unusual in that it has a pillar-shaped stone in the center and is divided into an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The front room is 3 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high, and the rear room is 3.6 meters long, 1.9 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. The corridor is 4.1meters long, 1.55 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included gold rings, gilt-bronze harnesses, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and based on these relics and the shape of the two stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]], it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 7th century, at the end of the Kofun period. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The excavated items are now kept at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. ==Gallery== File:Mitoshiro Kofun, 3D-B-1.jpg|3D rendition of burial chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-2.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-3.jpg|posterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, koushitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in posterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-6.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards back） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-7.jpg|anterior chamber（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, zenshitsu-sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus in anterior chamber File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-3.jpg|Corridor（looking towards entry） File:Mitoshiro Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor（looking towards anterior chamber） File:御年代古墳出土 子持平瓶.JPG|Sue pottery pottery from Mitoshiro Kofun
